hooks.prompt: A file was just written. Check the tool_input to see the file path. If the file path matches the pattern chapters/ch*.md (a chapter file), respond with: {"additionalContext": "CONTINUITY REMINDER: A chapter file was written. Ensure docs/continuity.md is updated for this chapter — either via the continuity-updater agent or manually."}. If the file is NOT a chapter file, respond with: {"additionalContext": ""}.

A file is about to be written. Check the tool_input file_path. If it matches docs/section_*_outline.md (a section outline file), check whether the file content contains 'Section status: authoritative' or similar authoritative marking. If it does, this is an authoritative section write. In that case, check: does docs/15_beats.md exist? If it does, does its header contain 'Beat status: Stable' or 'Beat status: Locked'? If beats are NOT at least Stable (they are Exploratory or Provisional), respond with: {"hookSpecificOutput": {"hookEventName": "PreToolUse", "permissionDecision": "deny", "permissionDecisionReason": "Beat status is not yet Stable. Authoritative section outlines require at least Stable beats. Mark the section as provisional, or run /stabilize-beats first."}}. For all other cases (not a section outline, section is provisional, or beats are stable/locked), respond with: {"hookSpecificOutput": {"hookEventName": "PreToolUse", "permissionDecision": "allow"}}.

Check the conversation transcript. Answer these questions: (1) Were any chapter files (chapters/ch*.md) written during this session? If yes, was docs/continuity.md also updated after each one? (2) Was a complete section finished (all chapters in a section written)? If yes, was a git commit made? (3) Are there any partially-written sections (some but not all chapters in a section written, with no indication the user wants to stop mid-section)? If all checks pass or no chapters were written, respond {"ok": true}. If any check fails, respond {"ok": false, "reason": "<specific description of what remains>"}.
